---
title: "TCGA"
author: Yuan Li (yuan.li@nbis.se)
format: html
---


```{r}
# BiocManager::install("TCGAbiolinks")
library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(parseMetab)
# remove.packages("parseMetab")
# detach("package:KinaseDownstream", unload = TRUE)
# remotes::install_github("Maj18/parseMetab", dependencies = TRUE)
# library(parseMetab)
```

```{r}
outdir = "../results/ImmuneScores/"
datadir = "../results/ImmuneScores/data"
dir.create(datadir, recursive=T, showWarnings=F)

```

# Download/import data

```
cd ../results/ImmuneScores/data
bash GetImmuneScores_TCGA.sh
```

## Read in immune scores for all TCGA samples to one single file
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
# 2. Find all .txt files matching your naming pattern
file_list <- list.files(
  path = datadir, 
  pattern = ".*_RNAseqV2\\.txt$", 
  full.names = TRUE
)

# 3. Read and combine all files into a single data.frame
combined_df <- file_list %>%
  # Use set_names to pass the file names down the pipeline
  set_names(basename(.)) %>%
  map_dfr(function(file_path) {
    # Read tab-separated file with specific column types
    read_tsv(
      file_path, 
      col_types = cols(
        ID = col_character(),
        Stromal_score = col_double(),
        Immune_score = col_double(),
        ESTIMATE_score = col_double()
      )
    )
  }, .id = "Disease_Type") %>%
  # Clean up the file name to extract just the disease name
  mutate(Disease_Type = sub("_RNAseqV2\\.txt$", "", Disease_Type))

# 4. View the structure and first few rows of your merged data
glimpse(combined_df)
head(combined_df)

# 5. Optional: Save your final master data.frame to a new CSV file
write_csv(combined_df, paste0(outdir, "TCGA_master_immune_scores.csv"))
# combined_df = readr::read_csv(paste0(outdir, "TCGA_master_immune_scores.csv"))

```

## Read in TCGA GSVA data

```{r}
# Import paired data meta
paired_data = readRDS("../results/TCGA/Summary/paired_data.RDS")
GSVA_limma_rslt = readRDS("../results/TCGA/Limma_GSVA/GSVAlimma_rslt_all.RDS")
# Add in immune score to meta data
meta = lapply(paired_data, function(d) {
    d[, c("sample_type", "sample.submitter_id", "cases.submitter_id", "project.x")] %>%
        mutate(Cancer_type = gsub("TCGA-", "", project.x)) %>%
        dplyr::select(-project.x) %>%
        as.data.frame()
}) %>% Reduce(rbind, .) %>% 
    mutate(Sample=sample.submitter_id) %>%
    # dplyr::select(-sample.submitter_id) %>%
    mutate(ID = stringr::str_sub(sample.submitter_id, 1, 15)) %>%
    # dplyr::select(-cases.submitter_id) %>%
    left_join(combined_df) 
# meta = meta[!is.na(meta$Immune_score),]


```

## Calculate spearman correlation between GSVA scores and Immune scores for cancer samples within each cancer type

```{r}
databaseDIR="../results/TCGA/KEGG_metabolism/"
kegg_metab_db_table = 
    readRDS(paste0(databaseDIR, "/KEGG_metabolism_db_table.RDS")) %>%
    dplyr::select(gs_name, class) %>%
    unique() %>%
    setNames(c("KEGG_Pathway", "Class")) %>%
    mutate(Class = paste0("KEGG_", Class))


# # correlation between Tumor-Normal (paired) and immune scores
# sharedCancers = intersect(meta$Cancer_type %>% unique(), names(GSVA_limma_rslt))
# lapply(sharedCancers, function(ct) {
#     print(ct)
#     sample1A = meta %>% 
#         filter(Cancer_type == ct) %>% 
#         filter(sample_type == "Primary Tumor") %>%
#         pull(sample.submitter_id) # tumor samples
#     sample2 = colnames(GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$gsva)
#     shared = intersect(sample1A, sample2)
#     print(shared)
#     if (length(shared)>2) {
#         patients = meta %>% 
#             filter(sample.submitter_id %in% shared) %>% 
#             pull(cases.submitter_id)
#         sample1B = meta %>% 
#             filter(cases.submitter_id %in% patients) %>% 
#             arrange(cases.submitter_id) %>%
#             pull(sample.submitter_id) %>%
#             setdiff(shared) # matched normal samples
#         print(sample1B)
#         rownames(meta) = NULL
#         meta_sub = meta %>% tibble::column_to_rownames(var="sample.submitter_id") 
#         immune_scores = meta_sub[shared, "Immune_score", drop=TRUE] %>% scale()
#         if (sum(!is.na(immune_scores))>2) {
#             gsvaFC = GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$gsva[, sample1A] - GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$gsva[, sample1B]
#             print(dim(gsvaFC))
#             print(length(immune_scores))
#             # Calculate Spearman correlation for each row
#             cor_results <- apply(gsvaFC, 1, function(row) {
#                 cor(row, immune_scores, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
#             }) 

#             # Convert the resulting named vector into a clean data.frame
#             correlation_df <- data.frame(
#                 Feature = names(cor_results),
#                 Spearman_Rho = cor_results,
#                 row.names = NULL
#             ) # %>% filter(Spearman_Rho>0.6)

#             classes = kegg_metab_db_table$Class %>% unique()
#             lapply(classes, function(class) {
#                 pathways = kegg_metab_db_table %>% 
#                     filter(Class == class) %>% pull(KEGG_Pathway)
#                 correlation_df %>% filter(Feature%in%pathways) %>% 
#                 pull(Spearman_Rho) %>% median(., na.omit=T)
#             }) %>% setNames(classes)
#         }
#     } else return(NA)
# }) %>% setNames(sharedCancers)

sharedCancers = intersect(meta$Cancer_type %>% unique(), names(GSVA_limma_rslt))
meta = meta[!is.na(meta$Immune_score),]
cor.list_full = lapply(sharedCancers, function(ct) {
    # print(ct)
    sample1 = meta %>% filter(Cancer_type==ct) %>% pull(sample.submitter_id)
    sample2 = colnames(GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$gsva)
    shared = intersect(sample1, sample2)
    if (length(shared)>0) {
        rownames(meta) = NULL
        meta_sub = meta %>% tibble::column_to_rownames(var="sample.submitter_id") 
        immune_scores = meta_sub[shared, "Immune_score"] %>% scale()
        gsva = GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$gsva[, shared]

        # Calculate Spearman correlation for each row
        cor_results <- apply(gsva, 1, function(row) {
            cor(row, immune_scores, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
        }) 

        # Convert the resulting named vector into a clean data.frame
        correlation_df <- data.frame(
            Feature = names(cor_results),
            Spearman_Rho = cor_results,
            row.names = NULL,
            Cancer_type = ct
        ) # %>% filter(abs(Spearman_Rho)>0.5)

        classes = kegg_metab_db_table$Class %>% unique()
        dat = lapply(classes, function(class) {
            pathways = kegg_metab_db_table %>% filter(Class == class) %>% pull(KEGG_Pathway)
            correlation_df %>% filter(Feature%in%pathways) %>% pull(Spearman_Rho) %>% median(., na.omit=T)
        }) %>% setNames(classes)
        return(correlation_df)
    } else {return(NA)}
}) %>% setNames(sharedCancers)
cor.list_full = cor.list_full[!is.na(cor.list_full)]

cor.list_full = cor.list_full[sapply(cor.list_full, function(cl) {nrow(cl)>0})] %>%
    Reduce(rbind, .) %>% mutate(KEGG_Pathway=Feature) %>%
    left_join(kegg_metab_db_table) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Carbohydrate metabolism","CAR",Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Lipid metabolism", "LIP", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of cofactors and vitamins", "COF", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Energy metabolism", "ENE", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Amino acid metabolism", "AMI", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Nucleotide metabolism", "NUC", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Biosynthesis of other secondary metabolites", "OSM", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of other amino acids", "OAA", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Glycan biosynthesis and metabolism", "GLY", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of terpenoids and polyketides", "TER", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Xenobiotics biodegradation and metabolism" , "XEN", Class)) %>%
            mutate(Class = gsub("KEGG_", "", Class))
openxlsx::write.xlsx(list(cor.list_full %>% select(-Feature)),
    paste0(outdir, "SpearmanCorWithImmuneScores_all.xlsx"))

cor.list = cor.list_full[sapply(cor.list_full, function(cl) {nrow(cl)>0})] %>%
    Reduce(rbind, .) %>% mutate(KEGG_Pathway=Feature) %>%
    filter(abs(Spearman_Rho)>0.5) %>%
    left_join(kegg_metab_db_table) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Carbohydrate metabolism","CAR",Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Lipid metabolism", "LIP", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of cofactors and vitamins", "COF", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Energy metabolism", "ENE", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Amino acid metabolism", "AMI", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Nucleotide metabolism", "NUC", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Biosynthesis of other secondary metabolites", "OSM", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of other amino acids", "OAA", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Glycan biosynthesis and metabolism", "GLY", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Metabolism of terpenoids and polyketides", "TER", Class)) %>%
            dplyr::mutate(Class = gsub("Xenobiotics biodegradation and metabolism" , "XEN", Class)) %>%
            mutate(Class = gsub("KEGG_", "", Class))

```

```{r}
p1 = ggplot(data=cor.list, mapping=aes_string(x="Cancer_type", y="Feature")) +
      geom_point(shape=21, aes(size=Spearman_Rho, fill=Spearman_Rho)) +
      # facet_wrap(~Condition, ncol=3) + 
      #ggtitle(title) +
      xlab("") +
      ylab("") + 
      # labs(size="p.adjust") +
      labs(size="Cor") +
      # scale_fill_manual(values=c("DOWN"="#6D6D6D", "UP"="#B32B16")) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 15) + 
      # scale_size(trans="reverse") +
      guides(alpha="none", size=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21))) +
      theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14)) # +
      # labs(caption=paste0("FDR < ", p.adjust.thresh))



nested=TRUE
color_low="skyblue"
color_high="purple"
cor.list$absRho = abs(cor.list$Spearman_Rho)
p = ggplot2::ggplot(data=cor.list, mapping=ggplot2::aes_string(x="Cancer_type", y="KEGG_Pathway")) +
        ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes_string(size="absRho", color="Spearman_Rho"))
    if (nested) p = p + ggh4x::facet_nested(Class~., scales = "free_y", space = "free")
    p = p + 
        ggplot2::ylab("") +  ggtitle("Cor: GSVA & Immune") +
        ggplot2::scale_color_gradient2(low=color_low, mid="snow2", high=color_high, midpoint=0) +
        # ggplot2::scale_color_gradient2(low="blue", mid="snow2", high="red", midpoint=0) +
        ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 15) + 
        ggplot2::guides(alpha="none", size=ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21))) +
        ggplot2::theme(
            axis.title =  ggplot2::element_blank(),
            strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, hjust=0.5),
            axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 90, hjust=1, vjust=0)) +
        ggplot2::labs(caption=paste0("Rho > 0.5"))
        
pdf(paste0(outdir, "SpearmanCorWithImmuneScores_Rho0.5.pdf"), h=16, w=10)
p
dev.off()

temp = cor.list %>% dplyr::select(-'absRho') %>% 
    dplyr::select(-Feature) 
openxlsx::write.xlsx(list(temp),
    paste0(outdir, "SpearmanCorWithImmuneScores_Rho0.5.xlsx"))

```

# Correlation between Rho (GSVA & immune scores) and Cancer vs Normal t values

```{r}
GSVA_limma_rslt = 
    readRDS("../results/TCGA/Limma_GSVA/GSVAlimma_rslt_all.RDS")
limma_rslt = lapply(names(GSVA_limma_rslt), function(ct) {
        dat = GSVA_limma_rslt[[ct]]$rslt_of_interest
        dat$Cancer_type = ct
        dat %>% tibble::rownames_to_column(var="KEGG_Pathway")
    }) %>% Reduce(rbind, .)
combined = cor.list_full %>% dplyr::select(-Feature) %>% left_join(limma_rslt)


cor.list_full = cor.list_full %>%
    mutate(Group="Immunometabolism") %>%
    mutate(absRho = abs(Spearman_Rho))

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
# Assuming your dataframe is named 'pathway_df'
pathway_correlations <- combined %>%
  group_by(KEGG_Pathway) %>%
  # Filter out pathways that don't have enough data points (minimum 3 cancers) to correlate
  filter(n() >= 3) %>% 
  summarise(
    # 1. Calculate the Spearman correlation coefficient (R)
    Spearman_Rho = cor(Spearman_Rho, t, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
    
    # 2. Extract the exact p-value safely using tryCatch
    P_Value = tryCatch({
      cor.test(Spearman_Rho, t, method = "spearman")$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_),
    
    # Track how many cancer types contributed to this pathway's correlation
    Number_of_Cancers = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # 3. Add an adjusted p-value (FDR) to control for testing multiple pathways
  mutate(Adj_P_Value = p.adjust(P_Value, method = "BH")) %>%
  # Sort by the strongest correlations
  arrange(desc(abs(Spearman_Rho)))

# View the final correlation lookup table
print(pathway_correlations)
pathway_correlations = pathway_correlations %>%
    left_join(., combined[,c("KEGG_Pathway", "Class")]) %>%
    mutate(Cancer_type = "Immunometabolic-Coupling") %>%
    mutate(Group = "IMC")
pathway_correlations$absRho = abs(pathway_correlations$Spearman_Rho)
rm(p)

# Combine the two correlations
long.table = rbind(pathway_correlations[, 
    c("Cancer_type", "KEGG_Pathway", "Group", "absRho", "Spearman_Rho", "Class")],
    cor.list_full [, 
    c("Cancer_type", "KEGG_Pathway", "Group", "absRho", "Spearman_Rho", "Class")])

drawCorDotplot = function(long.table, absRho_cutoff=0){
    nested=TRUE
    color_low="skyblue"
    color_high="purple"
    # 1. Base ggplot initialization remains the same
    p = ggplot2::ggplot(data=long.table %>% filter(absRho>absRho_cutoff), 
        mapping=ggplot2::aes_string(x="Cancer_type", y="KEGG_Pathway")) +
        ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes_string(size="absRho", color="Spearman_Rho"))

    # This single command completely splits your rows by Class and your columns by Group
    p = p + ggh4x::facet_nested(Class ~ Group, scales = "free", space = "free")


    # 3. Add styling themes and descriptors
    p = p + 
        ggplot2::ylab("") +  
        ggplot2::ggtitle("Cor: Metabolism & Immune") +
        ggplot2::scale_color_gradient2(low=color_low, mid="snow2", high=color_high, midpoint=0) +
        ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 15) + 
        ggplot2::guides(alpha="none", size=ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21))) +
        ggplot2::theme(
            axis.title   = ggplot2::element_blank(),
            # Styled strip text for x-axis panels (Group)
            strip.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
            strip.text.y = ggplot2::element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, hjust=0.5),
            axis.text.x  = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 90, hjust=1, vjust=0.5) # Tweaked vjust to align text nicely
        ) +
        ggplot2::labs(caption=paste0("|Rho| > ", absRho_cutoff))

    return(p)
}

p1 = drawCorDotplot(long.table, absRho_cutoff=0)       
pdf(paste0(outdir, "SpearmanCorWithImmuneScores_all.pdf"), h=19, w=12)
p1
dev.off()

p2 = drawCorDotplot(long.table, absRho_cutoff=0.5)       
pdf(paste0(outdir, "SpearmanCorWithImmuneScores_absRho_cutoff0.5.pdf"), h=19, w=12)
p2
dev.off()




```


```{r}
save.image(paste0(outdir, "/TCGA.RData"))
# load(paste0(outdir, "/TCGA.RData"))
out = capture.output(sessionInfo())
writeLines(out, paste0(outdir, "/sessionInfo.txt"))

```
